Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: First looking at all cases I should calculate the total time from ticket receive to close Case 101 runs from 2024-03-01 08:00 to 10:15 on the same day that is about 2 hours 15 minutes Case 103 is similar from 08:10 to 09:30 so 1 hour 20 minutes These seem pretty fast Now cases 102 104 and 105 span multiple days which immediately stands out Case 102 starts at 08:05 and closes the next day at 09:15 so roughly 25 hours Case 104 is from 08:20 to the next day 08:30 about 24 hours 10 minutes Case 105 takes even longer from 08:25 to the day after that at 09:30 which is about 49 hours So clearly cases 102 104 and 105 are significantly slower

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Now whats causing these delays Let me check each steps timing In case 102 after assignment at 09:00 theres a big jump to escalation at 11:30 that is 2.5 hours just waiting Then another wait until investigation at 14:00 and then overnight before resolution the next day The escalation to Level-2 seems to add a lot of delay Case 104 has a similar issue After assignment at 09:30 investigation doesnt start until 13:00 that is 3.5 hours of waiting Then resolution happens the next morning No escalation here but that wait time is huge Case 105 shows escalation happening after initial investigation but then a long wait until Level-2 investigates the next day and resolution another day later So escalations definitely correlate with longer times

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Also I notice that in the faster cases like 101 and 103 the steps flow quickly with minimal delays For instance in 103 from assignment to investigation is only 15 minutes and resolution follows soon after So the bottleneck seems to be either in the investigation stage or after escalation

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Maybe theres an issue with agent availability or ticket complexity Cases that need escalation might be harder but the delays suggest that either Level-2 agents arent available quickly or theres a handoff problem Also the long waits before investigation could indicate that agents are overloaded or that tickets arent being prioritized properly

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: So recommendations could include better triage to catch escalations earlier ensuring Level-2 agents are more responsive and maybe adjusting workload or improving handoff processes between teams Also monitoring wait times between steps could help identify bottlenecks earlier
